notes: Provisional compatibility ceilings copied from the cited historical reference run. cmd/eval enforces them fail-closed when -budgets is supplied, but the current harness measures a different workload (all 12 Stable operations, degree-stratified symbols, semantic checks, post-Stable-suite MAXRSS). They are not comparable post-change ratchets. Replace them only from a new current-harness ubuntu-latest run on the current commit; never rewrite historical reports.

hero_suite.notes: The historical run measured <1ms (millisecond-granularity floor) for every hero task on the tier-1 fixtures, so these zero values carry no latency ratchet signal. The current correctness gate is the full scenario pass rate; performance requires a new current-harness reference run.

real_repos.notes: These values came from the previous harness and are retained only as fail-closed upper compatibility limits. In those reports impact was absent from the structural pool and peak_rss_mb was sampled immediately after IngestAll; the 11821 MB guava value therefore cannot be attributed to agent_brief or Stable cache reads. The current harness includes impact, degree-stratified sampling, semantic checks, and stable_peak_rss_mb after all Stable operations. Until a new green ubuntu-latest matrix run is pinned, current comparable latency/RSS baselines and the cause of the historical guava peak are UNKNOWN.
